top_stories.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
top_stories.summary: A technical analysis details two attack techniques—ConsentFix and ClickFix—that hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by stealing OAuth tokens and bypassing MFA within seconds. The attacks exploit fake consent prompts and OAuth flows to gain persistent access without requiring user credentials.

top_stories.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
top_stories.summary: A new infostealer dubbed 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials that connect development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

top_stories.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
top_stories.summary: The article examines how traditional Identity Governance and Administration (IGA) frameworks are structurally misaligned with the requirements of AI agents as autonomous principals in enterprise environments. It identifies specific gaps in identity lifecycle management models—such as the absence of employment records, managers, and departure dates—that existing IGA tools were not designed to address.

top_stories.title: Managing local administrator passwords with Windows LAPS and Microsoft Intune
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Intune has integrated with Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) to automate local administrator account password management on enrolled devices. The integration uses the Windows LAPS Configuration Service Provider (CSP) to handle local account security, a capability relevant to privileged access management (PAM) practices.

top_stories.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
top_stories.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "ARToken" has been identified as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing platform, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The toolkit is designed to bypass authentication controls and compromise Microsoft 365 credentials and session tokens.

news.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
news.summary: Jamf Threat Labs discovered a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that impersonates the Maccy clipboard manager and abuses PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) checks to steal Mac login passwords. The malware is distributed as a compiled AppleScript file and leverages PAM mechanisms to siphon sensitive authentication credentials from infected systems.

news.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
news.summary: Ransomware groups linked to the Anubis operation are exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 vulnerability (CVE-2025-5777) to gain initial access to target environments. The attack patterns include credential access techniques, use of legitimate RMM tools, and supply chain credential abuse for lateral movement.

news.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
news.summary: Kaspersky has attributed a new malware called Umbrij to the threat actor ToddyCat, which abuses OAuth to gain unauthorized access to Gmail accounts via the Google API. The malware targets corporate email communications by compromising API-level access rather than using traditional credential theft methods.

news.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
news.summary: A credential theft campaign called "FortiBleed" targeting FortiGate devices has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations. Stolen credentials from the campaign were intended for follow-on ransomware intrusions, with an operator linked to the infrastructure found actively working negotiation panels for both ransomware groups.

news.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
news.summary: A 19-year-old alleged Scattered Spider member, Peter Stokes, has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud. Scattered Spider is a hacking group known for social engineering and identity-based attacks targeting corporate help desks and authentication systems.

news.title: Microsoft Purview unifies insider risk alerts and user context for faster triage
news.summary: Microsoft has updated Purview Insider Risk Management with a unified alert experience that consolidates classic alerts and agent-generated insights into a single queue. The update is designed to streamline security team workflows by combining insider risk alerts with user context for faster triage of potential internal threats.

news.title: Teleport Earns Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year
news.summary: Teleport has received the Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year. The award recognizes Teleport's Infrastructure Identity platform for its zero trust architecture, which secures AI agents, humans, machines, and workloads.

news.title: BIO-key Showcases Passwordless & Biometric Identity Solutions at Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th
news.summary: BIO-key will showcase its passwordless and biometric identity and access management solutions at a Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th. The event marks BIO-key's participation in a regional banking sector cybersecurity event focused on biometric-powered IAM technologies.

news.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
news.summary: Microsoft has announced it is accelerating its post-quantum cryptography roadmap with a target of 2029, citing faster-than-expected advances in quantum computing that threaten existing encryption standards. Azure CTO Mark Russinovich stated that quantum research developments have shifted the risk horizon, making earlier replacement of current encryption standards necessary.

news.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
news.summary: A large-scale automated password spray attack targeting Microsoft Azure CLI has compromised at least 78 accounts across more than 81 million attempts between June 12 and June 26. The attack originates from an IPv6 address range controlled by infrastructure provider LSHIY LLC and was identified by Huntress researchers.

news.title: Show HN: Identity Layer for Agents and Autonomous AI
news.summary: A developer has released an open-source identity layer project for AI agents and autonomous AI systems, published on GitHub under authsec-ai/authsec-ai. The project implements OAuth 2.1, CIBA, SPIFFE workload identity for agent-to-agent authentication, a secret vault, and MFA.

news.title: Microsoft Security June 2026: new features and record Patch Tuesday
news.summary: Microsoft's June 2026 security updates introduce new identity backup and recovery capabilities alongside AI-driven vulnerability scanning and expanded multicloud coverage. The release is part of a broader Patch Tuesday update affecting Microsoft's security portfolio.

news.title: Anthropic Adds Enterprise Gateway to Simplify Claude Code Access on AWS and Google Cloud
news.summary: Anthropic has released an enterprise gateway for Claude Code that includes SSO, central policy management, and spend caps on Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud. The SSO feature is an access control component of a broader AI developer tooling product rather than a dedicated IAM solution.

news.title: Microsoft Intune June 2026: App auto-updates, privilege controls, and enrollment improvements
news.summary: Microsoft Intune's June 2026 release introduces new features including Enterprise Application Management auto-updates reaching general availability and new privilege controls. The update also includes device enrollment improvements relevant to enterprise access and endpoint management workflows.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add Non-Human Identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to manage and secure machine identities, API keys, service accounts, and other non-human credentials as part of its broader security platform.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used in high-profile breaches.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions circulating on Telegram demonstrated how to socially engineer the AI bot into bypassing normal authentication controls to perform unauthorized password resets.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
